Summary of testing:
Tests performed (name of test and test
clause):

Clause Remarks Information/Comments
1.3 Collateral standards The equipment has been evaluated also according to
collateral standard IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 +
AMD2:2020, see attachment 3.
4.2 Risk management process RM Results are documented by the manufacturer on the
basis of ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 14971:2019/A11:
2021 referring to the OD-2044-1 (ed.1.0) from IECEE,
and are reviewed by Nemko.
4.8 Components The power supply box, model DEPS9601 and
DEPS9601A have been separately tested and certified by
Nemko (CBTC No. NO82590/A1/M2 and NO96755/M1)
according to IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012, refer to
appended table 8.10 and attachment 5.
The AC/DC adapter, model BM090S15Fhas been
separately tested and certified by UL (CBTC No. DK-
51428-A1-M1-UL) according to IEC 60601-1:2005 +
AMD1:2012, refer to appended table 8.10 and
attachment 6.
The rechargeable Li-ion battery pack, model ELT-3S1PA
has been separately tested and certified by UL (CBTC
No. DK-70440-M1-UL) according to IEC 62133:2012,
refer to appended table 8.10 and attachment 7.
These test report were reviewed and found to be
acceptable. Acceptance of this power supply box is at the
discretion of the accepting NCB or agency.
6 Classification The equipment has the following classifications:
- Type of protection against electric shock:
● Class I for power supply box and AC/DC adapter
● Internally powered for detector
- Degree of protection against electric shock (Patient
connections): Type B applied part for front panel of
detector
- Degree of protection against harmful ingress of water or
particulate matter: IP54 for detector
- Methods of sterilization: The equipment is not intended
to be sterilized by operator.
- Degree of safety application for use in an oxygen
environment: Not applicable.
- Mode of operation: Continuous operation
7 Identification, markings and
documents
Markings and instructions related to the safety shall be in
a language which is acceptable in the country in which
the equipment is to be sold. Markings and instructions in
English were evaluated in this test report.

compliance with the requirements of ISO 10993-1.
Biocompatibility is not evaluated as part of this test
report.

medical systems (PEMS)

compliance with the requirements of clause 14, and that
the software is classified as Class B to IEC 62304.
Compliance is documented by the manufacturer and
reviewed by Nemko. See also "Attachment - Software -
IEC 62304:2006 + AMD1:2015" in this test report.

compliance with the requirements of IEC 60601-1-2,
Electromagnetic disturbances. Compliance is
documented by the manufacturer.

General product information and other remarks:
This test report No. REP028549 replaces the previous original test report No. REP001824 due to
updating the standard version to IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 to IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012
+ AMD2:2020, and covers technical modifications as following:
- Updating the standard version by adding AMD2:2020 to IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013
(see attachment 3).
- Changing the address of applicant, manufacturer and factory (see bold letters on page 1,
13 and copy of marking plate).
The equipment is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic
diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based
radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists.
It is not to be used for mammography.
The equipment consists of power supply box, digital flat panel X-ray detector, switch box and exposure
switch. The power supply box and digital flat panel X-ray detector are intended for use with patient
environment. The switch box and exposure switch are intended for use outside patient environment.
The equipment provides two kinds of power configuration, refer to following configuration for each power
configuration:
- Power configuration 1: Power supply box, data cable (rating of detector: 3 A, 16 Vdc)
- Power configuration 2: AC/DC adapter, ethernet cable (rating of detector: 6 A, 15 Vdc)

Page 14 of 192 Report No. REP028549
TRF No. IEC60601_1U
General product information and other remarks: (Continued)
The equipment is generally used with X-ray equipment / system including operating PC in end-use
application. Refer to below set-up diagrams which are intended by the manufacturer.
- AED mode (Without switch box)
- Normal mode (with switch box)
Based on above set-up diagrams, further consideration shall be checked as following when the equipment
is used in the end-use application.
- The suitability of the mounting means shall be tested and evaluated.
- The compliance shall be tested and evaluated when the equipment constitutes as ME system in the
end-use application.
- X-ray equipment / system shall be powered from separate mains supply which complies with IEC
60601-1.
- Operation PC shall be powered from separate mains supply which complies with IEC 60601-1 or
IEC 60950-1.

All testing was conducted with the PC submitted by manufacturer (Type: Latitude, manufactured by Dell
Inc.), since it doesn’t provide in the product package, Therefore, the equipment should be considered
whether the compliance needs to be additionally tested evaluated, when it constitutes as the ME system
with other equipment which is not verified in this test report.

Page 15 of 192 Report No. REP028549
TRF No. IEC60601_1U
General product information and other remarks: (Continued)
The equipment provides the battery pack recharger and recharger adapter in the product package. Those
components are intended to be used outside of patient environment and certified according to IEC 60950-
1. Therefore, those components are not tested and evaluated in this test report.
Model differences:
- The main model is A1717MCW.
- The variants A1417MCW and F1417MCW are identical to main model A1717MCW except for
model name, dimension, weight, total pixel number, total pixel area, effective pixel number,
effective pixel area, material of fire enclosure, main board and gate board as below table:

Supplementary Information:
1) Multiplication factor (1.14 for MOOP) is applied for altitude up to 3000 m based on table 8.
2) Highest working voltage, minimum measured creepage and minimum measured air clearance are
taken from the power supply box, see attachment 5.
3) Short-circuit testing was considered due to the insufficient insulation distance. Leakage current testing
was performed during short-circuit in order to show no hazard in this area, see appended table 8.7.
4) Highest working voltage, minimum measured creepage and minimum measured air clearance are
taken from the AC/DC adapter, see attachment 6.

TRF No. IEC60601_1U
INSULATION DIAGRAM CONVENTIONS and GUIDANCE:
A measured value must be provided in the value columns for the device under evaluation. The
symbol > (greater than sign) must not be used. Switch-mode power supplies must be re-evaluated in
the device under evaluation therefore N/A must not be used with a generic statement that the
component is certified.
Insulation diagram is a graphical representation of equipment insulation barriers, protective
impedance and protective earthing. If feasible, use the following conventions to generate the
diagram:
- All isolation barriers are identified by letters between separate parts of diagram, for example separate
transformer
windings, optocouplers, wire insulation, creepage and clearance distances.
- Parts connected to earth with large dots are protectively earthed. Other connections to earth are functional
- Applied parts are extended beyond the equipment enclosure and terminated with an arrow.
- Parts accessible to the operator only are extended outside of the enclosure but are not terminated with an
arrow.

Supplementary information:
Observer, with a visual acuity of 0 on the log Minimum Angle of Resolution (log MAR) scale or 6/6 (20/20) and is able to
read N6 of the Jaeger test card in normal room lighting condition (~500lx), reads marking at ambient illuminance least
favourable level in the range of 100 lx to 1,500 lx. The me equipment or its part was positioned so that the viewpoint was
the intended position of the OPERATOR or if not defined at any point within the base of a cone subtended by an angle of
30° to the axis normal to the centre of the plane of the marking and at a distance of 1 m.
1) Recommended in the user manual.

clause 8.6.4 b).
5) Testing was conducted after short-circuited between secondary circuit and applied part (refer to area E
and K on insulation diagram) due to insufficient insulation distance.
6) Testing was conducted with detector powered from rechargeable Li-ion battery (Battery operated).

B - Before humidity conditioning

0 - Switch open or set to reversed polarity
NC - Normal condition
SFC - Single fault condition

- Motor operated ME EQUIPMENT - least favourable voltage between 90 % of the minimum RATED and 110 %

- Combined heating and motor operated and other ME EQUIPMENT - tested both at 110 % of the maximum
RATED voltage and at 90 % of the minimum RATED voltage.

effects. Also, see instructions for use.

adding it to the maximum allowed ambient temperature (35 °C) specified in the technical description.

8) Estimated duration for contact: 10 s ≤ t < 1 min.

4) Supply voltage:
- ME EQUIPMENT with heating elements - 110 % of the maximum RATED voltage;

- Motor operated ME EQUIPMENT - least favourable voltage between 90 % of the minimum RATED and 110 %
of the maximum RATED voltage. ME EQUIPMENT operated under normal load and normal DUTY CYCLE.
- Combined heating and motor operated and other ME EQUIPMENT - tested both at 110 % of the maximum
RATED voltage and at 90 % of the minimum RATED voltage.
5) APPLIED PARTS intended to supply heat to a PATIENT - See RISK MANAGEMENT FILE containing temperatures and clinical
effects. Also, see instructions for use.
- Testing was conducted with power supply box, model DEPS9601A (power configuration 1).
6) The measured temperature is determined by measuring the temperature rise of the part under test and
adding it to the maximum allowed ambient temperature (35 °C) specified in the technical description.
7) Estimated duration for contact: 1 s ≤ t < 10 s.
8) Estimated duration for contact: 10 s ≤ t < 1 min.
9) Estimated duration for contact: 10 min ≤ t.

3) When thermocouples used to determine temperature of windings, limits of Table 22 reduced by 10 °C.
4) Supply voltage:
- ME EQUIPMENT with heating elements - 110 % of the maximum RATED voltage;
- Motor operated ME EQUIPMENT - least favourable voltage between 90 % of the minimum RATED and 110 %
of the maximum RATED voltage. ME EQUIPMENT operated under normal load and normal DUTY CYCLE.
- Combined heating and motor operated and other ME EQUIPMENT - tested both at 110 % of the maximum
RATED voltage and at 90 % of the minimum RATED voltage.
5) APPLIED PARTS intended to supply heat to a PATIENT - See RISK MANAGEMENT FILE containing temperatures and clinical
effects. Also, see instructions for use.
- Testing was conducted with power supply box, model DEPS9601A (power configuration 1).
6) The measured temperature is determined by measuring the temperature rise of the part under test and
adding it to the maximum allowed ambient temperature (35 °C) specified in the technical description.

Attachment - Software – IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 —
4.3 [A, B, C] Software safety classification —
a) The MANUFACTURER assigns to each SOFTWARE
SYSTEM a software safety class according to the RISK
of HARM to the patient, operator, or other people
resulting from a HAZARDOUS SITUATION to which the
SOFTWARE SYSTEM can contribute in a worst-case-
scenario
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1
P
The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class A if: —
– the SOFTWARE SYSTEM not contribute to a
HAZARDOUS SITUATION; or
Classified as software class B. N/A
– the SOFTWARE SYSTEM contribute to a HAZARDOUS
SITUATION which does not result in unacceptable
RISK after consideration of RISK CONTROL measures
external to the SOFTWARE SYSTEM
N/A
The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class B if: —
– the SOFTWARE SYSTEM contribute to a HAZARDOUS
SITUATION which results in unacceptable RISK after
consideration of RISK CONTROL measures external to
the SOFTWARE SYSTEM and the resulting possible
HARM is non-SERIOUS INJURY
P
The SOFTWARE SYSTEM is software safety class C if: —
– the SOFTWARE SYSTEM contribute to a HAZARDOUS
SITUATION which results in unacceptable RISK after
consideration of RISK CONTROL measures external to
the SOFTWARE SYSTEM and the resulting possible
HARM is death or SERIOUS INJURY
Classified as software class B. N/A
For a SOFTWARE SYSTEM initially classified as
software safety class B or C, the MANUFACTURER
has implemented additional RISK CONTROL measures
external to the SOFTWARE SYSTEM and subsequently
has assigned a new software safety classification to
the SOFTWARE SYSTEM
N/A
c) The MANUFACTURER documents the software
safety class assigned to each SOFTWARE SYSTEM in
the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1
P
d) When a SOFTWARE SYSTEM is decomposed into
SOFTWARE ITEMS, and when a SOFTWARE ITEM is
decomposed into further SOFTWARE ITEMS, such
SOFTWARE ITEMS inherit the software safety
classification of the original SOFTWARE ITEM (or
SOFTWARE SYSTEM) unless the MANUFACTURER
documents a rationale for classification into a
different software safety class
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1
P
A rationale explains how the new SOFTWARE ITEMS
are segregated so that they may be classified
separately
. P

Page 176 of 192 Report No. REP028549
IEC 60601-1
Clause Requirement + Test Result - Remark Verdict
TRF No. IEC60601_1U
e) The MANUFACTURER documents the software
safety class of each SOFTWARE ITEM if that class is
different from the class of the SOFTWARE ITEM from
which it was created by decomposition
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1
P
f) When applied to a group of SOFTWARE ITEMS, the
MANUFACTURER uses the PROCESSES and TASKS
which are required by the classification of the
highest-classified SOFTWARE ITEM in the group
unless the MANUFACTURER documents in the RISK
MANAGEMENT FILE a rationale for using a lower
classification
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1
P

SYSTEM, until a software safety class is assigned
Classified as software class B. N/A

7 SOFTWARE RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS —
7.1 Analysis of software contributing to hazardous situations —
7.1.1 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER identifies SOFTWARE ITEMS
that could contribute to a hazardous situation
identified in the MEDICAL DEVICE RISK ANALYSIS
ACTIVITY of ISO 14971
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
7.1.2 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER identifies potential causes
of the SOFTWARE ITEM identified above contributing
to a hazardous situation
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
The MANUFACTURER considers potential causes including, as appropriate: P
a) incorrect or incomplete specification of
functionality
P
b) software defects in the identified SOFTWARE ITEM
functionality
P
c) failure or unexpected results from SOUP N/A
d) hardware failures or other software defects that
could result in unpredictable software operation
P
e) reasonably foreseeable misuse P
7.1.3 [B, C] If failure or unexpected results from SOUP is a
potential cause of the SOFTWARE ITEM contributing to
a hazardous situation, the MANUFACTURER
EVALUATES as a minimum any ANOMALY list
published by the supplier of the SOUP item relevant
to the VERSION of the SOUP item used in the MEDICAL
DEVICE to determine if any of the known ANOMALIES
result in a sequence of events that could result in a
hazardous situation
N/A
7.1.4 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER documents in the RISK
MANAGEMENT FILE potential causes of the SOFTWARE
ITEM contributing to a hazardous situation
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
7.2 RISK CONTROL measures —
7.2.1 [B, C] For each case documented in the RISK
MANAGEMENT FILE where a SOFTWARE ITEM could
contribute to a HAZARDOUS SITUATION, the
MANUFACTURER defines and documents RISK
CONTROL measures in accordance with ISO 14971
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
7.2.2 [B, C] If a RISK CONTROL measure is implemented as part of the functions of a
SOFTWARE ITEM, the MANUFACTURER:
P

Page 186 of 192 Report No. REP028549
IEC 60601-1
Clause Requirement + Test Result - Remark Verdict
TRF No. IEC60601_1U
a) includes the RISK CONTROL measure in the
software requirements
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
b) assigns to each SOFTWARE ITEM that contributes
to the implementation of a RISK CONTROL measure a
software safety class based on the RISK that the
RISK CONTROL measure is controlling
P
c) develops the SOFTWARE ITEM in accordance with
Clause 5
P
7.3 VERIFICATION of RISK CONTROL measures —
7.3.1 [B, C] The implementation of each RISK CONTROL
measure documented in 7.2 is VERIFIED, and this
VERIFICATION is documented
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
The MANUFACTURER reviewers the RISK CONTROL
measure and determines if it could result in a new
HAZARDOUS SITUATION
P
7.3.3 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER documents TRACEABILITY of software HAZARDS as
appropriate:
P
a) from the hazardous situation to the SOFTWARE
ITEM
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 11
P
b) from the SOFTWARE ITEM to the specific software
cause
P
c) from the software cause to the RISK CONTROL
measure
P
d) from the RISK CONTROL measure to the
VERIFICATION of the RISK CONTROL measure
P
7.4 RISK MANAGEMENT of software changes —
7.4.1 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER analyses changes to the MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE
(including SOUP) to determine whether:
N/A
a) additional potential causes are introduced
contributing to a hazardous situation
N/A
b) additional software RISK CONTROL measures are
required
N/A
7.4.2 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER analyses changes to the
software, including changes to SOUP, to determine
whether the software modification could interfere
with existing RISK CONTROL measures
N/A
7.4.3 [B, C] The MANUFACTURER performs relevant RISK
MANAGEMENT ACTIVITIES defined in 7.1, 7.2 and 7.3
based on these analyses
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P

9 SOFTWARE PROBLEM RESOLUTION PROCESS —
9.1 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER prepares a PROBLEM
REPORT for each problem detected in the MEDICAL
DEVICE SOFTWARE
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
PROBLEM REPORTS include a statement of criticality
(for example, effect on performance, SAFETY, or
SECURITY) as well as other information that may aid
in the resolution of the problem (for example,
devices affected, supported accessories affected)
P
9.2 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER: P
a) investigates the problem and if possible identify
the causes
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
b) EVALUATES the problem’s relevance to SAFETY
using the software RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS
P
c) documents the outcome of the investigation and
evaluation
P
d) creates a CHANGE REQUEST(S) for actions needed
to correct the problem, or document the rationale for
taking no action
P
9.3 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER advises relevant
parties of the existence of the problem, as
appropriate
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
9.4 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER approves and
implements all CHANGE REQUESTS, observing the
requirements of the change control PROCESS
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
9.5 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER maintains records of
PROBLEM REPORTS and their resolution including their
VERIFICATION
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
The MANUFACTURER updates the RISK MANAGEMENT
FILE as appropriate
P
9.6 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER performs analysis to
detect trends in PROBLEM REPORTS
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
9.7 [A, B, C] The MANUFACTURER verifies resolutions to determine whether: P
a) problem has been resolved and the PROBLEM
REPORT has been closed
Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
b) adverse trends have been reversed P
c) CHANGE REQUESTS have been implemented in the
appropriate MEDICAL DEVICE SOFTWARE and
ACTIVITIES
P
d) additional problems have been introduced P
9.8 [A, B, C] When testing, retesting or REGRESSION TESTING SOFTWARE ITEMS and
SYSTEMS following a change, the MANUFACTURER includes in the test documentation:
P
a) test results Software validation report
(A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3
P
b) ANOMALIES found P

Exception:
Fixed and stationary X-ray ME EQUIPMENT supplied
from a branch circuit rated at 30 A or less, and ME
EQUIPMENT that is not strictly portable but obviously
is intended to be stationary, may be acceptable if
provided with a length of attached hard service
flexible cord - such as Type S, or the equivalent,
for supply connection.
N/A
The installation of connecting cords between
EQUIPMENT parts meets the requirements of the
NEC, as applicable. Cable used as external
interconnection between units are as follows:
N/A
1) If exposed to abuse, the cables are Type SJT,
SJTO, SJO, ST, SO, STO, or equivalent flexible
cord or similar multiple-conductor appliance-wiring
material such as computer cable
N/A
2) If not exposed to abuse, the cables are as
indicated in item 1) above or are:
i) Type SPT-2, SP-2, or SPE-2, or equivalent,
ii) Type SVr, SVRO, SVE, or equivalent flexible
cord or similar multiple-conductor appliance
wiring material, or
iii) An assembly of insulated wires each with a
nominal insulation thickness of 0.8 mm (1/32 inch)
or more, enclosed in acceptable insulating tubing
having a nominal wall thickness of 0.8 mm (1/32
inch) or more.
N/A
Receptacles provided as part of ME EQUIPMENT or
ME SYSTEMS for use in the patient care areas of
paediatric wards, rooms, or areas are listed
tamper resistant or employ a listed tamper
resistant cover in accordance with the NEC.
N/A
b) For ME EQUIPMENT provided with NEMA
configuration non-locking plug types 120 V/15 A,
125 V/20 A, 250 V/15 A, 250 V/20 A “Hospital
Grade” mains plug is provided and the POWER
SUPPLY CORD is marked.

8 Protection against electrical HAZARDS from ME EQUIPMENT
8.6 Protective earthing, functional earthing and potential equalization of ME
EQUIPMENT
P
8.6.4 Impedance and current-carrying capability P
[Replace Clause 8.6.4 in the adopted Standard, as modified by IEC Amendments 1
and 2, with the following]
P
PROTECTIVE EARTH CONNECTIONS shall be
able to carry fault currents reliably and without
excessive voltage drop.
P
Impedance and current-carrying capability shall
comply with CSA C22.2 No. 0.4. P
For PERMANENTLY INSTALLED ME
EQUIPMENT and ME EQUIPMENT with a non-
DETACHABLE POWER SUPPLY CORD, the
impedance between the PROTECTIVE EARTH
TERMINAL (inside the ME EQUIPMENT)
and any part that is PROTECTIVELY EARTHED
shall not exceed 100 mΩ. For ME EQUIPMENT
with an APPLIANCE INLET, the impedance
between the earth pin of the APPLIANCE INLET
and any part that is PROTECTIVELY EARTHED
shall not exceed 100 mΩ…………………………..:
See appended table 8.6.4 in
part 1 original test report No.
REP028549.
P
In addition to the test above, for ME EQUIPMENT
with a non-DETACHABLE POWER SUPPLY
CORD or any DETACHABLE POWER SUPPLY
CORD (supplied or specified by the
MANUFACTURER), the impedance between the
protective earth pin of the MAINS PLUG and the
PROTECTIVE EARTH TERMINAL (inside the ME
EQUIPMENT) shall not exceed 100 mΩ………….:
See appended table 8.6.4 in
part 1 original test report No.
REP028549.
P
Where an APPLIANCE INLET forms the supply
connection to ME EQUIPMENT, the earth pin of
the APPLIANCE INLET is regarded as the
PROTECTIVE EARTH TERMINAL.
The combined testing requirements above are
equivalent to 200 mΩ impedance testing
requirements as described in IEC 60601-1.
Separate testing is required to comply with CSA
C22.2 No. 0.4.
P

Attachment 2
National Deviations
Page 12 of 33
Report No.: REP028549
IEC60601_1U ATTACHMENT
Clause Requirement + Test Result - Remark Verdict
Testing shall be carried out using a DETACHABLE
POWER SUPPLY CORD as provided or specified
(length and cross-sectional area as per the
Canadian Electrical Code, Part I) by the
MANUFACTURER.
P
The test current shall have the following
characteristics:
— for cord-connected equipment, twice the rating
of the attachment plug cap, but not less than 40 A;
— for equipment for permanent connection to the
supply, twice the rating of the fuse that is required
by the Canadian Electrical Code, Part I for the
branch circuit to which the equipment is
connected, up to 250 A; and
— 500 A for equipment for permanent connection
to the supply when a branch circuit fused at over
250 A is required.
See appended table 8.6.4 in
part 1 original test report No.
REP028549.
P
Compliance is checked by the following test:
— for test currents up to 500 A, the measured
potential drop shall not exceed 4 V;
— for equipment that requires branch circuit fusing
over 250 A, the measured potential drop multiplied
by the required fusing and divided by 250 shall not
exceed 4 V;
— there shall be no melting of any metal in the
bond and no heating or burning that is likely to
create a fire hazard; and
— the time duration— the time duration for testing
is indicated in Table 8.6.4A:
N/A
P
Alternatively, dc may be used for this test, if the
ME EQUIPMENT is rated dc. N/A

Attachment 2
National Deviations
Page 13 of 33
Report No.: REP028549
IEC60601_1U ATTACHMENT
Clause Requirement + Test Result - Remark Verdict
Note: When protective earth is relied on as a
MEANS OF PROTECTION, the test current is
determined based on the location where a fault
could occur. If the prospective fault is in the mains
supply circuit prior to the overcurrent protection
included in the ME EQUIPMENT, the test current
for that part of the protective earth circuit is based
on the rating of the external overcurrent protection
included in the building infrastructure or specified
in the ACCOMPANYING DOCUMENTS (two times
the interrupt rating of the external overcurrent
protection). If the prospective fault is in the mains
supply circuit after the overcurrent protection
included in the ME EQUIPMENT, the
test current is based on the rating of the
overcurrent protection included in the ME
EQUIPMENT (two times the interrupt rating of the
ME EQUIPMENT overcurrent protection). In either
case, the minimum test current is 40 A.
The voltage drop between the parts described is
measured and the impedance determined from the
current and voltage drop.
If the measured impedance is within the permitted
limit, either the impedance measurement is then
repeated using a current source with a no-load
voltage sufficient to deliver the specified current
into the total impedance, or the current-carrying
ability of the relevant protective earth conductor
and protective earth connection is confirmed by
checking that their cross-sectional area is at least
equal to that of the relevant current-carrying
conductors.
N/A

N/A
Note 1: As a separating transformer is not a MAINS SUPPLY TRANSFORMER, it does not
require more than BASIC INSULATION.
Note 2: Limitation of output power is not explained in CAN/CSA-E61558-2-1 and the
RATED output power is defined by the fuse in the installation and by the allowable
power supply cable used. However, the characteristics of the separating transformer
need to be carefully selected, taking into account the variations in the load current of
the ME SYSTEM to ensure that the voltage supplied to the various items of the ME
SYSTEM remains within the limits specified for the equipment.
Note 3: For additional details refer to the Canadian Electrical Code, Part I,
Diagrams 1 and 2.

Use of uncertainty of measurement for decisions on conformity (decision rule) :
No decision rule is specified by the IEC standard, when comparing the measurement result with the
applicable limit according to the specification in that standard. The decisions on conformity are made
without applying the measurement uncertainty (“simple acceptance” decision rule, previously known as
“accuracy method”).
Other: … (to be specified, for example when required by the standard or client, or if national
accreditation requirements apply)
Information on uncertainty of measurement:
The uncertainties of measurement are calculated by the laboratory based on application of criteria given
by OD-5014 for test equipment and application of test methods, decision sheets and operational
procedures of IECEE.
IEC Guide 115 provides guidance on the application of measurement uncertainty principles and applying
the decision rule when reporting test results within IECEE scheme, noting that the reporting of the
measurement uncertainty for measurements is not necessary unless required by the test standard or
customer.
Calculations leading to the reported values are on file with the NCB and testing laboratory that conducted
the testing.

authorized by the respective NCBs that own these marks.

4 PRINCIPLES
4.1.1 The MANUFACTURER shall establish, document,
implement and maintain a USABILITY
ENGINEERING PROCESS, as defined in Clause
5, to provide SAFETY for the PATIENT, USER and
others. The PROCESS shall address USER
interactions with the MEDICAL DEVICE according
to the ACCOMPANYING DOCUMENTATION
Usability validation report (A-
TCF-5-107)
P
USABILITY ENGINEERING activities for a
MEDICAL DEVICE shall be planned, carried out,
and documented by personnel competent on the
basis of appropriate education, training, skills or
experience.
Usability engineering report (A-
TCF-5-108)
P
Where a documented product realization PROCESS
exists, such as that described in Clause 7 of ISO
13485:2016, it shall incorporate the appropriate
parts of or reference the USABILITY ENGINEERING
PROCESS .
Usability engineering report (A-
TCF-5-108)
P
Compliance with this subclause to exist when the
requirements of this International Standard have
been fulfilled.
P
4.1.2 To reduce use-related RISK, the
MANUFACTURER shall use one or more of the
following options, in the priority listed (as required
by ISO 14971:2019, 7.1):
Usability validation report (A-
TCF-5-107)
P
a) inherently safe design and manufacture; P
b) protective measures in the MEDICAL DEVICE
itself or in the manufacturing PROCESS; and
P
c) information for SAFETY and, where appropriate,
training to USERS.
P
4.1.3 When, in accordance with the priorities of 4.1.2,
information for SAFETY is used as a RISK CONTROL
measure, the MANUFACTURER shall subject this
information to the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS
Usability validation plan (A-
TCF-5-106)
Usability validation report (A-
TCF-5-107)
P
4.2 The results of the USABILITY ENGINEERING PROCESS
are stored in the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE ............. :
Usability engineering report (A-
TCF-5-108)
P

tools used to perform the USABILITY ENGINEERING
PROCESS vary based on:
Usability engineering report (A-
TCF-5-108)
P
a) the size and COMPLEXITY of the USER INTERFACE P
b) the SEVERITY of the HARM associated with the use
of the MEDICAL DEVICE
P
c) the extent or complexity of the USE SPECIFICATION P
d) the presence of USER INTERFACE OF UNKNOWN
PROVENANCE
P

based on other circumstances specific to the
MEDICAL DEVICE and the MANUFACTURER
P
A summary of any selection scheme, the rationale
for its use and the results of applying it shall be
stored in the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE
Document Reference No. in
USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE :

TCF-5-108), chapter 5.5

discovered during this step the MANUFACTURER
shall repeat the steps of Clause 5 as appropriate
P
If training on the specific MEDICAL DEVICE is required
for the safe us of the MEDICAL DEVICE by the
intended USER, the MANUFACTURER shall design and
implement a training capability for the EXPECTED
SERVICE LIFE of the MEDICAL DEVICE by doing at
least one of the following:
P
- provide the materials necessary for training; P
- ensure the materials necessary for training are
available;
P
- make the training available; or P
- make training available to the RESPONSIBLE
ORGANIZATION that enables it to train its USERS
P

selected in Clause 5.5

determined

1. Safety Information
1.1 Notice
For the improvement of Product performance, supplementation and the follow-up of
information the contents of this manual are subject to change without prior notice.
Please, note that our company bears no responsibility for accidental damage nor will we be
obligated to provide warranty service for any damage to equipment due to user error. Please,
follow the instructions in this manual closely. Become familiar with the safety precautions and
usage procedures for this Product. Note that the Product may differ slightly from the contents
of this manual, depending on individual Product specifications.
This manual is written in English. This manual is for the EU region and translated based on the
English manual. If other EU language translation is required, please refer to the manual
information and contact the manufacturer's customer service or a local distributor.

1.3 Warning
Symbols Descriptions
Environment of Use and Storage
⚫ Follow the specified process of operation instruction written in this
manual for the safety of the users and patients.
⚫ Local regulations should be reviewed before using to the pregnant or
those who have limitations in X-rays due to health issues. Make sure
to check the conditions of X-ray irradiation in advance.
Connection
⚫ Do not connect the detector with anything other than specified.
Otherwise, it may result in fire or electric shock.
⚫ To avoid the risk of electric shock, this detector must only be
connected to supply mains with protective earth.
⚫ Avoid installing the system in such way that it is difficult for the
operator to disconnect the appliance plug.
⚫ Do not touch Signal Input Part/Signal Output Part and the patient
simultaneously. There is a risk of electric shock from leakage current.
⚫ Additional equipment connected to medical electrical equipment
must comply with the respective IEC or ISO standards. Furthermore,
all configurations shall comply with the requirements for medical
electrical systems. Anybody connecting additional equipment to
medical electrical equipment configures a medical system and is
therefore responsible that the system complies with the requirements
for medical electrical systems. Attention is drawn to the fact that local

11
laws take priority over the above-mentioned requirements.
⚫ Equipment connected to the detector and in the patient, environment
must be powered from a medically-isolated power source or must be
a medically-isolated device. Equipment powered from a non-isolated
source can result in chassis leakage currents exceeding safe levels.
Chassis leakage current created by an accessory or device connected
to a non-isolated outlet may add to the chassis leakage current of the
detector.
⚫ Do not install at the building power system prospective short-circuit
current exceeding 35 A.
⚫ The x-ray systems and console software used with the product must
be CE certified.
Handling
⚫ Always be sure to keep checking the condition of the system and the
patient to ensure they are normal during the use of the detector. If
any problem is found, take appropriate measures, such as stopping
the operation of the detector, as required.
⚫ Never disassemble or modify the detector as it may result in fire or
electric shock.
⚫ Also, since the detector incorporates parts that may cause electric
shocks and other hazardous parts, touching them may cause death or
serious injury.
⚫ Do not hit or drop the detector. The detector may be damaged if it
receives a strong jolt, which may result in fire or electric shock if the

12
detector is used without being repaired.
When Problem Occurs
⚫ Should any of the following occur, immediately remove the battery
from the detector, unplug the power supply cord from the AC outlet,
and contact H&abyz Customer Service team or authorized distributor.
➢ When there is smoke, odd smell or abnormal sound.
➢ When liquid has been spilled into the detector or a metal object
has entered through an opening.
➢ When the detector has been dropped and it is damaged.
⚫ If there are no side effects but other problems occur, please contact
H&abyz customer service center.
Error Massages
⚫ Messages about occur errors when using the detector are not output
directly from the software.
⚫ It is provided in text format for use in upper-level software (see
Section 1.5).
⚫ For error message contents and actions, refer to the relevant upper-
level software manual.

13
⚫ If the Upper-Level Software Manual does not work, contact your
H&abyz Customer Service team or authorized dealer.
Maintenance and Inspection
⚫ For safety reasons, be sure to turn off the power of the detector
when the following inspections are going to be performed. Otherwise,
it may result in electric shock.
➢ Make sure to turn off the detector, remove the battery, and
unplug the power cable from the AC outlet before cleaning.
➢ Do not use any type of solvent, such as benzene. Otherwise, fire
or electric shock may result.
➢ Wear waterproof gloves to protect your hands from direct
contact with IPA (Isopropyl-alcohol) or any other liquid.
➢ Maintenance of the detector should be done by an authorized
service provider. If problem still cannot be corrected, it may result
in fire or electric shock.
Security
⚫ The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW protects all the transmitted
data by applying such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2, WPA2PSK,
WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.
⚫ The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW restricts random access by
setting ID / PW when connecting to wireless network.
⚫ In the event of a threat related to the cybersecurity of the equipment
and software, immediately stop the equipment or software from

14
working and contact the manufacturer or distributor to take the
necessary action.
Patients Contact
⚫ The effective area surface with a decoration sheet of the detector is
available for direct patient contact.

15
1.4 Caution
Symbols Descriptions
User Requirements
⚫ This equipment should be only operated by users who have received
professional medical education and training, such as physicians,
radiologists, and other medical specialists.
⚫ Federal law (USA) restricts this device to sale by or on the order of a
physician.
Environment of Use and Storage
⚫ Do not install the detector in a location with the conditions listed
below. Otherwise, it may result in failure or malfunction, cause fire or
injury.
➢ Close to facilities where water is used.
➢ Where it will be exposed to direct sunlight.
➢ Close to air-conditioner or ventilation equipment.
➢ Close to heat source such as a heater.
➢ Prone to vibration.
➢ Dusty environment.
➢ Saline or sulfurous environment.
➢ High temperature or humidity.
⚫ Do not place the storage case in a location with the conditions listed
below.
➢ Where someone might get their foot caught in the cable.

16
➢ Where the cable of the detector unit will be strongly pulled when
the detector is put into the case, otherwise, the cable may be
damaged, resulting in fire or electric shock.
⚫ Make sure to avoid prolonged contact with a patient for safe use.
(Max.10 min). Highest temp of the surface: 45 ℃ (when the
surrounding temp is 35 ℃)
Handling
⚫ In order to prevent infection, please wipe the CFRP (Carbon Fiber
Reinforced Plastic) with a soft cloth moistened with IPA (Isopropyl-
alcohol) liquid. Wear waterproof gloves to protect your hands from
direct contact with IPA or any other liquid.
⚫ The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are rated IP56 of water-
resistant and dustproof according to IEC 60529 standard. Water and
dust may damage the detector, so please pay attention to the
following information.
➢ Whenever your device gets wet, dry it thoroughly with a clean,
soft cloth.
➢ Do not expose the detector to salt water or ionized water.
➢ The IP56 of water-resistant and dustproof is only guaranteed
when the battery cover is closed tightly while the wired
connection cable is disconnected and connected to wireless
mode.
➢ Battery cover is consumable parts, so these parts should be
replaced periodically after more than 2500 use. (3 times/day per

17
week, expected 3 years).
➢ Covers may be loosened slightly if the detector is dropped or
receives an impact. Ensure that all the covers are properly
aligned and tightly closed. Otherwise, they may not provide
protection from water and dust. Waterproof performance cannot
be guaranteed if shock/load is applied beyond the allowable
specifications of the product.
⚫ For safety’s sake, keep all the cables unplugged and the battery
removed when the detector is not in use.
⚫ Make sure to use the battery in the conditions mentioned below.
Otherwise, it may result in a fire, injury, failure, or malfunction.
➢ Be sure to recharge the battery by using the detector or the
battery recharger provided by H&abyz.
➢ If the battery is needed to be stored for a long period, battery
should be removed from the application and stored in a place
where humidity and temperature are low.
➢ While the battery is charged, used and stored, keep it away from
object materials with static electric chargers.
➢ If the terminals of the battery become dirty, wipe with a dry cloth
before using the battery.
➢ Be sure to use the battery in the environment designated by
H&abyz. Otherwise, the life of battery can be short.
➢ Stop using the battery if the battery becomes abnormally hot,
disorder, discoloration, deformation, or abnormal conditions is

18
detected during use, charge, or storage.
➢ Don't short circuit (+) and (-) terminals with metallic object
intentionally.
➢ Not be sure to touch liquid on the surface of the battery.
➢ Don't pierce the battery with a sharp object such as a needle,
screw drivers.
➢ Do not hit the battery with heavy objects.
➢ Don't step on the battery and throw or drop the battery on the
hard floor to avoid mechanical shock.
➢ Don't disassemble the battery or modify the battery design
including electric circuit.
⚫ Make sure to use the battery in the conditions mentioned below.
Otherwise, it may result in a fire, injury, failure, or malfunction.
➢ When using battery recharger, always use the battery provided by
H&abyz.
➢ When using battery recharger, be sure to use Adapter Provided
by H&abyz.
➢ When moving battery recharger, always separate the power and
battery pack.
➢ When using battery recharger, always be cautious about touching
liquid, impurities. In case of short trouble.
➢ Do not disassemble the battery recharger.
➢ When using battery recharger, be sure to comply with the user

19
environment. The battery provided by H&abyz can be down and
the durability can be shorter.
➢ Be sure to use battery recharger in the Control Room. This
battery recharger is not designed to use in the Examination
Room.
➢ When not using the battery recharger for a long time. Always do
separate the battery recharger from the Power.
➢ When inserting the battery to the battery recharger, be careful of
being caught clothes or impurities.
Location of Cables
⚫ Make sure all cables are located so that they cannot be stepped on,
tripped over, or otherwise subjected to damage or stress.
Maintenance and Inspection
⚫ For safety reasons, be sure to inspect the detector before using it. In
addition, carry out a regular inspection at least once a year.
⚫ If the detector is defective, do not disassemble the detector randomly.
Maintenance of the detector should be done by an authorized service
provider. Please contact H&abyz Customer Service team or authorized
agent.
⚫ Be sure to check the user’s manual for replaceable components.

20
1.5 Safety Information
⚫ General Safety
Preparation

➢ Be sure to connect the cables to the proper connectors. Otherwise, the detector may
malfunction or may be damaged.
➢ The battery should be equipped at a specified location. Otherwise, the detector may
malfunction or be damaged.
Handling
➢ Handle the detector carefully, as it may become damaged if it is hit, dropped, or
receives a strong jolt.
➢ Be sure to use the detector on a flat place so it will not bend. Otherwise, the detector
may be damaged.

21
➢ Be sure to check the detector daily and confirm that it works properly. Sudden
heating of the room in cold areas will cause condensation to form on the detector. In
this case, wait until condensation disappears before performing exposure. If the
detector is used with condensation formed on it, problems may occur in the quality
of the detector. When an air-conditioner is going to be used, be sure to raise/lower
the temperature gradually so that a difference in temperature in the room and in the
detector does not occur, to prevent forming of condensation. Follow the
recommended proper room temp.
➢ Do not use the detector near devices generating a strong magnetic field. Doing so
may produce image noise or artifacts.
➢ Keep the connectors free from being in contact with the patient.
➢ Connectors are intended to be connected to an external device and must follow IEC
standards.
➢ Do not apply excessive weight to the detector. Otherwise, the detector may be
damaged.
➢ Use a Weight Distribution Cap when weight of a patient is concentrated on one
particular point.
<Uniform Load: uniform distribution of weight>

22
<Local Load: concentration of weight >
Disinfection and Cleaning
➢ Do not spray disinfectants or detergents on the detector.
➢ Make sure to turn off the detector, remove the battery and unplug the power cable
from the AC outlet before cleaning.
➢ Do not use flammable chemicals such as benzene for cleaning. Otherwise, there is a
risk of a fire.
➢ Wear waterproof gloves to protect your hands from direct contact with disinfectants
or detergents.
⚫ Electrical Safety
➢ In compliance with IEC 60601-1, this medical equipment complies with the
requirements for Safety class I.
➢ This system must be used in an environment complying with the requirements of the
IEC.
⚫ EMC (Electromagnetic Compatibility)
➢ Do not use any wireless devices, including cellular phones, near the system. All

23
wireless devices, whether they comply with the EMC standard or not, may emit
electromagnetic interferences and cause the system to malfunction when they are
used nearby.
➢ See Section (10. EMC Declaration).
⚫ Error messages for higher-level software
“Connect Error”
➢ Description: connection unavailable between the detector and the software.
➢ Cause: network disconnection or power supply removal.
➢ Solution: In case of connection error, the “Connect Error” message is passed to the
upper-level software. Check the detector power supply and network cable
connection. If necessary, replace the power supply, power cable and network cable.

24
“Image Processing Error”
➢ Description: Unremovable noise on the acquired image when pre-processing.
➢ Cause: when the provided calibration file is changed or removed, a number of noise
or line is occurred on the acquired image.
➢ Solution: Check the provided calibration file and if it is changed or removed,
reobtain the calibration file and apply.
[ figure 1 – 1 error image ]
[ figure 1 – 2 normal image ]

2. Product Introduction and Specification
2.1 Product Features
The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that
have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems. The
detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read
through it into digital signals. The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology
that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.
The flat panel sensors of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are fabricated using thin film
technology based on amorphous silicon technology. Electronically, the sensors are much like
conventional photodiode arrays. Each pixel in the array consists of a light‐sensing photodiode
and a switching Thin Film Transistor (TFT) in the same electronic circuit. The amorphous silicon
photodiode is sensitive to visual light, and its response curve has almost the same

28
performance as human vision. The sensitivity of amorphous silicon photodiodes peaks in green
wavelengths, well matched to scintillators such as CsI. It also has excellent linearity according
to the exposure dose.
Intended purpose
The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW (Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector) is indicated as a
digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human
anatomy targeting both adults and children. It is intended to replace film-based radiographic
diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.
Not to be used for mammography.
Indications
It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise
case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.
Contraindications
Local regulations should be reviewed before using those who have limitations in X-rays due to
health issues. Make sure to check the conditions of X-ray irradiation in advance.
Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application.
Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.
Target patient group
Adult and children except pregnant women.

29
Life cycle of product
About 5 years.
Applied part and duration of application
Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

30

2.3 Performance Principle
The X-ray output from the X-ray generator passes through the object to be photographed and
is then converted into a digital signal through the detector to be output to the image signal.
X-rays weaken in dense tissue such as bones, and soft tissue uses the principle of easy passage
to adjust the brightness of the image to express the signal.
The indirect type detector consists of a Scintillator for converting X-rays into visible light and a
TFT panel for converting them into data. A TFT array consists of covered with a thin layer of
silicon that is amorphous or disordered, and silicon is engraved with millions of transistors
arranged in higher order like a grid of graph paper. Each of these thin-film transistors (TFTs) is
attached to a light-absorbing photodiode that makes up individual pixels, and visible light

34
entering the photodiode is converted by intensity into a digital signal and output to the screen
in a readable digital image.
2.4 Intended patient population
Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular,
hand, foot and other internal organs.
a) Age: Adult and Children
b) Weight: not relevant
c) Height: not relevant
d) Nationality: not relevant
e) Patient group/state: except pregnant women
d) Applications parts: Part of body
f) Disease: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal,
abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs
2.5 Conditions of intended user knowledge
a) Education: at least have the ability to read the manual
b) Knowledge: User must be read and understand user manual.
c) Understanding of languages: Language displayed on the label or user manual
d) Experience: Radiologist
e) Allow disorders:
• Mild reading eyesight impairment or corrected eyesight on log MAR 0.2(6/10 or
20/32)
• Equipment along the one touch or grasp the arm / hand
• Average degree of age-related short-term memory damage

35
• 40% damage due to normal hearing ranging from 500Hz to 2KHz.
2.6 Specification of Device
a) Rated voltage: (100-240) V~
b) Rated frequency: 50/60 Hz
c) Electric power consumption: MAX 48W
d) Protection type: Class I or internally powered
e) IP (Ingress Protection) Range: IP56
f) The degree of protection against electric shock: Type B applied part for front panel of
digital flat panel X-ray detector
g) Adapter fuse: Built-in overload protection circuit
h) The method(s) of sterilization: Not applicable
i) The degree of safety of application in the presence of a FLAMMABLE ANAESTHETIC
MIXTURE WITH AIR or WITH OXYGEN OR NITROUS OXIDE: Not applicable
j) Mode of operation: As momentary continuous operation

3. Installation and Calibration
3.1 Installation
⚫ Software Installation
➢ Run “XAS_W_Setup.exe” file to install “XAS_W” and “EzView”, and click “Next”.
➢ Click “Install” to start installation.

59
➢ Click “OK’ when the installation is finished.
➢ Microsoft Visual Studio Package is necessary to use Abyz Acquisition Software and
Viewer. Check “Accept” and click “Install”.

60
➢ Click “Close” when the installation is finished.
➢ Click “OK” when XAS_W_Setup is completed.

61
⚫ Product Set-Up
1) Connection methods of each operation mode
➢ AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode, Not use switch box)
The user can acquire images without additional connection between the X-ray system,
which is already installed, and the detector. When X-rays are exposed by an exposure
switch of the installed X-ray system, the detector detects the X-rays that are absorbed
without any additional external exposure signals and convert them into digital signals.
Converted digital data is delivered to PC via AP (Access Point) or main cable, and PC
converts the delivered data into images to show it on the monitor.
※ Object, PC, X-ray System are not included in this component.

62
➢ Normal Mode (with Switch Box)
The user can acquire images by connecting Switch Box to X-ray system that is already
installed. Normal Mode is to send exposure signals to PC and X-ray System using
Switch Box and a separate exposure switch. By pressing the exposure switch connected
to Switch Box, the detector is activated and ‘Ready’ sign can be seen. When prepared,
the detector delivers exposure signals to X-ray System.
The detector converts absorbed x-ray to digital data, delivers it to PC via AP (Access
Point) or main cable, and PC converts the delivered digital data into images to show it
on the monitor.
※ Object, PC, X-ray System and Control panel are not included in this component.
• The detector operates in a wireless state when using battery power
alone, and in a wired state when using a data cable.

63
2) PC Set-Up
➢ Set-Up the Network as below
[Network Connection] > [Local Area Connection] (Click the right mouse button) >
Click “Properties”
➢ Select [Internet Protocol Version 4(TCP/IP)] > Click “Properties”

64
➢ Select [Use the following IP address] > Enter the IP address the Detector IP address.
• H&abyz Detector’s default IP address is 192.168.197.80, Also you can
use 192.168.197.80 ~ 192.168.197.200 detector IP. H&abyz
recommends 192.168.197.2 ~ 192.168.197.70 for PC IP address and
255.255.255.0 for Subnet mask.

65
⚫ Image Acquisition software (XAS_W) set up
➢ Connect the detector and turn the power on.
➢ If the connected detector IP address is different from the IP address, enter the
detector IP address connected to the "Detector IP Address" and click "set" button.

66
➢ Run “XAS_W.exe” which is in “C:\XAS_W”. Once the detector is connected to PC
successfully, the network info sign shall turn from red ‘X’ to green ‘✓’
➢ Verify that the software "Com Port" is set to the same value after verifying the port
number of the Switch Box USB cable connected to the PC in the Device Manager.
➢ After checking connection, select Trigger Mode (Trigger Mode) out of Normal Mode
and AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode). If it is successful, a pop-up shall

67
come on and the changed Trigger Mode (Trigger Mode) be shown in “Detector
Status”.
3.2 Calibration
Calibration shall be performed to acquire a proper image. The image will not be clear enough
without calibration.
• Calibration shall be performed once every one year for stabilized
detector condition.

68
⚫ Dark image acquisition
➢ Click "Get Dark" to get a Dark image on the "Calibration" tab. The "Preview" window
appears and acquires the Dark image. The acquired Dark image will be displayed in
the image file list.
• H&abyz recommends to use the "Average Dark" option to enhance
image performance.
• If you select the "Average Dark" option, Dark images will be
automatically acquired to the number of "total dark frame" values.
• Dark image in AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode) is saved
in the below location.
C:\XAS_W\Cal_0\CalType0-AED\
• Dark image in Normal Mode is saved in the below location.
C:\ XAS_W \Cal_0\CalType0-NorTrg\

69
⚫ Bright image acquisition and creation of calibration image
➢ According to the Trigger Mode (Operation Mode), the bright image is acquired in
different ways.
➢ In AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), click the “Get Bright” button of the
software to acquire the bright image. The bright images must be acquired at twenty
calibration points. The acquired bright images are shown in the image file list.

70
➢ In Normal Mode, without clicking the “Get Bright” on the software and uses the
Switch Box Exposure switch to acquire the bright image. The bright images must be
acquired at twenty calibration points. The acquired bright images are shown in the
image file list.

71
• H&abyz recommends twenty calibration points such as written below.
Some artifact can be seen on images if the calibration points are not
sufficient.

※ Cal point 1 ~ 17: with Al 21mm filter
※ Cal point 18 ~ 20: without Al 21mm filter

• In AED mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), each bright image
must be acquired at intervals of 30 seconds.
• In AED mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), X-ray shall be
exposed within 30 seconds once the “Acquisition” button is clicked.
Otherwise, the action will be cancelled.
• In Normal Mode, the image is acquired using Switch Box Exposure
switch, not clicking “Get Bright” button.
• Do not expose X-ray when “Busy” message is displayed in “Preview”
pop-up window. Any Images can’t be acquired during “Busy” state
although X-ray is exposed. Expose X-ray when “Ready for Exposure”
is shown up in “Preview” window.

➢ When acquiring the bright image, the name of the image file must be Bright01.raw ~
Bright20.raw. When you create the bright image file, the calibration image
"*_OC_xxxx.raw" is automatically created. The acquired bright images are shown in
the image file list.

⚫ BPM Generation

BPM.

4. Direction for Use
4.1 Set-up
⚫ Set-up of Image
➢ Optionally, pre-processing options can be selected. But select the options as shown
below.
• When three pre-processing options in “Acquisition” tap are selected,
corrected images can be acquired.

76
⚫ Set-up of Detector
➢ Designate the file that has the parameter value for detector setup.
➢ For A1717MCW detector, select A1717MCW.par parameter.
➢ For A1417MCW detector, select A1417MCW.par parameter.
➢ For F1417MCW detector, select F1417MCW.par parameter.

77
➢ Set the rotor delay.
① Rotor delay means the time between the prep switch and the exposure switch.
1.0 sec is the default setting.

78
⚫ Multi Detector Set-up
H&abyz detector support “Multi Detector” function to connect a several detectors to one
PC. This function is useful when two detectors are integrated in an X-ray system.
• it could take a few seconds to change to another detector according
to PC condition. If customers repeatedly select others during
changing the detector, all detectors can be disconnected.
➢ Click “Edit” in “Setting” tap.

79
➢ Click “Add”. Then other detectors that you want to add will be shown in “Detector
List”.
➢ Enter the names of the additional detectors in “Alias” and change IP address, image
size, calibration file location, and image file location and click “Apply”. Once the
changes are applied, click “Confirm”.

80
➢ Make sure the additional detectors are shown as below and select necessary
detectors.

81
4.2 Image Acquisition
⚫ Image Acquisition Method
➢ Check the network connection and required pre-processing options are selected in
“Acquisition” tab, and click the “Enable Cal Engine” checkbox. Check the
“Cal_handle=*” message appears in the “Detector Status” message window.

82
➢ Click the “Pre-Processing” check box. Enter the image file name in “Image Name”.

83
➢ In AED Mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), click the “Get Bright” button of the
software to acquire the bright image. The acquired bright images are shown in the
image file list.

84
➢ In Normal Mode, without clicking the “Get Bright” on the software and uses the
Switch Box Exposure switch to acquire the bright image. The acquired bright images
are shown in the image file list.
• In AED mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), each bright image
must be acquired at intervals of 30 seconds.
• In AED mode (Auto Exposure Detection Mode), X-ray shall be
exposed within 30 seconds once the “Acquisition” button is clicked.
Otherwise, the action will be cancelled.
• In Normal Mode, the image is acquired using Switch Box Exposure
switch, not clicking “Get Bright” button.
• Do not expose X-ray when “Busy” message is displayed in “Preview”
pop-up window. Any Images can’t be acquired during “Busy” state

85
although X-ray is exposed. Expose X-ray when “Ready for Exposure”
is shown up in “Preview” window.

7. Maintenance
7.1 Cleaning
⚫ Clean the detector with 70% IPA (Isopropyl-alcohol) when it is contaminated.
⚫ Before cleaning the detector, turn off the power and separate the battery.
⚫ Wear waterproof gloves to protect your hands from direct contact with IPA or any other
liquid.
⚫ Do not pour or spray IPA directly on the detector. Use fabric or soft cloth moistened with
IPA to clean.
⚫ Avoid getting IPA or any other liquid into the detector.
⚫ After cleaning, wait until the IPA is dried completely.
7.2 Inspection
⚫ In order to ensure that the detector is used safely and normally, please be sure to inspect
the product regularly before use.
⚫ Please perform inspections based on the check list below.
Inspection List User Vendor Cycle
Check if cables are not damaged √ Daily
Check if plugs and connectors are not connected or
damaged √ Daily
Check if cover or part is not damaged √ Daily
Check the LED indicator √ Daily
Re-Calibration √ Yearly

99
7.3 Replaceable Parts and Instruction of Replacement
⚫ Fuse: T6.3AH/250V (5x20mm)
➢ Press the buttons in the red box below to take the fuse box out.
➢ Remove two fuses from the fuse box and replace them with others.
⚫ LAN Cable: UTP 4P 27AWG (CAT.7, straight-through)

100
⚫ AC power Cable: 250 V~, 10 A, 0.75mm2 x 3C (KR Spec.)

device components.
Disposal of old Electrical & Electronic Equipment.
This symbol indicates that this product shall not be treated as household waste. Instead, it shall
be handed over to the applicable collection point for the recycling of electrical and electronic
equipment. By ensuring this product is disposed of correctly, you will help prevent potential
negative consequences for the environment and human health, which could otherwise be caused
by inappropriate waste handling of this product. For more detailed information about recycling
this product, please refer to local governing ordinances and recycling plans.

9. Warranty
9.1 Warranty procedure
If Buyer promptly notifies H&abyz or Seller regarding any parts that fail to perform as specified
under normal usage during the Warranty Period and H&abyz determines that such failure resulted
from a defect in materials or workmanship during the Warranty Period, then H&abyz, at its option,
shall repair, rebuild or adjust the affected parts.
H&abyz is not responsible for defects that occur in the following environments:
(i) Normal and fair wear and tear or Product which has been modified without H&abyz’s
approval.
(ii) Product which has not been installed in strict conformity to the H&abyz’s directions or
used an input power source that is different from the input power source specified in
the user manual, or damaged by improper handling, storage or use by buyer or a third
party.
(iii) Modification of the software of the product by the buyer or a third party.
(iv) Use of Product in combination with devices or products not purchased from H&abyz.
(v) Use or application of Product in a field or in an environment for which such Product was
not designed or contemplated.
(vi) Use of any parts or material not provided by H&abyz for warranty service.
(vii) The third party’s maintenance not certified by H&abyz.
(viii) Force majeure such as natural disaster.
H&abyz shall not, in any event or under any circumstances, be responsible for damages or
other sums in excess of the total purchase price actually paid by Buyer to Seller i.e., H&abyz or
H&abyz’s authorized agent. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing under no
circumstance shall H&abyz be responsible or liable in any regard with respect to damages from
loss of use, loss of time, loss of data, inconvenience, commercial loss, lost profits or savings, or
other incidental, special or consequential damages claimed by Buyer to arise out of the use or

103
inability to use the Product, even if Buyer has been advised of the possibility of such damages.
In the event that the product is returned to H&abyz after the warranty has expired, H&abyz
reserves the right to invoice a reasonable fee for the repair services provided to Buyer.

H&abyz shall make the sole final determination about whether the fail to perform occurred in
normal usage (under warranty) or not (excluded from warranty). If the authorized agent or the
Buyer doesn’t accept the result of H&abyz’s investigation, the burden of proof is on them.
9.2 Warranty
If Buyer needs to make a claim based on this Warranty, Buyer should advise Seller in writing
immediately at the following address:
Product Name Digital Flat-Panel X-ray Detector
Model Name A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW
Life Time 5 years from sales date
Warranty 3 years from sales date

10. EMC Declaration
10.1 Compliance Statement
This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
This equipment needs special precautions regarding EMC and needs to be installed and put
into service according to the EMC information provided in the user manual.
Do not use any wireless devices, including cellular phones, near the system. All wireless devices,
whether they comply with the EMC standard or not, may emit electromagnetic interferences and
cause the system to malfunction when they are used nearby.
This equipment should not be used adjacent to or stacked with other equipment; if adjacent or
stacked use is necessary, the equipment should be tested and verified in order to ensure normal
operations in the configuration in which it will be used.
Changes or modifications not expressly approved by the party responsible for compliance could
void the user's authority to operate the equipment.
All interconnect cables to peripheral devices must be shielded and properly grounded, except
when technologically prohibited. Use of cables not properly shielded and grounded may result in
the equipment causing radio frequency interference.
The use of cables or accessories other than those specified may result in increased emissions or
decreased immunity.
Should any interference (EMC) be detected with other equipment, please position other
equipment away from this one.
When using this system on a patient with an implantable pacemaker or an implantable
cardioverter defibrillator, the patient must be informed that continuous X-ray exposure in pulse
form may cause the pacemaker or cardioverter defibrillator to malfunction.
When using this system, ensure that X-rays are not exposed directly onto the patient’s
implantable pacemaker or implantable cardioverter defibrillator, and that the exposure time is kept
as short as possible.

105
This equipment may be interfered with by other equipment, even if that other equipment
complies with CISPR EMISSION requirements.
10.2 Guidance and Manufacturer’s Declaration – Electromagnetic Emissions
This product is intended for use in the electromagnetic environment specified below. The
customer or the user of this product should assure that it is used in such an environment.
This Wireless Detector is intended for use in the electromagnetic environment specified below. The
user of this Wireless Detector should assure that it is used in such an environment

a Field strength from fixed transmitters, such as base stations for radio (cellular/cordless) telephones

predicted theoretically with accuracy. To assess the electromagnetic environment due to fixed RF
transmitters, an electromagnetic site survey should be considered. If the measured field strength in
the location in which this Wireless Detector is used exceeds the applicable RF compliance level
above, this Wireless Detector should be observed to verify normal operation. If abnormal
performance is observed, additional measures may be necessary, such as re-orienting or relocating
this Wireless Detector.
b Over the frequency range 150 kHz to 80 MHz, field strengths should be less than 3 V/m.
c The ISM (industrial, scientific and medical) bands between 150 kHz and 80 MHz are 6.765 MHz to
6.795 MHz; 13.553 MHz to 13.567 MHz; 26.957 MHz to 27.283 MHz; and 40.66 MHz to 40.70 MHz..

10.4 Recommended separation distances between portable and mobile RF
communications equipment and this Wireless Detector
This Wireless Detector is intended for use in an electromagnetic environment in which radiated
RF disturbances are controlled. The customer or the user of this Wireless Detector can help
prevent electromagnetic interference by maintaining a minimum distance between portable
and mobile RF communications equipment (transmitters) and this Wireless Detector as
recommended below, according to the maximum output power of the communications
equipment.
Rated maximum

separation distance d in meters (m) can be estimated using the equation applicable to the
frequency of the transmitter, where P is the maximum output power rating of the transmitter in
watts (W) according to the transmitter manufacturer.
NOTE 1 At 80 MHz and 800 MHz, the separation distance for the higher frequency range
applies.
NOTE 2 These guidelines may not apply in all situations. Electromagnetic propagation is
affected by absorption and reflection from structures, objects and people.

6 Classification of ME Equipment
and ME systems
Equipment has been evaluated as Class I, continuous
operation, ordinary equipment and has not been evaluated
for use in the presence of flammable anaesthetic mixture
with air, oxygen, or nitrous oxide. The equipment is not
intended for patient connection.

Calibration All instruments used in the tests given in this test report are calibrated and
traceable to national or international standards.
Further information about traceability will be given on request.
Measurement
uncertainty
Measurement uncertainties are calculated for all instruments and instrument
set-ups given in this report. Calculations are based on the principles given in
the standard EA-4/02 (Dec. 1999), IEC Guide 115:2007 and other relevant
internal Nemko-procedures.
Further information about measurement uncertainties will be given on request.
Evaluation of results If not explicitly stated otherwise in the standard, the test is passed if the measured
value is equal to or below (above) the limit line, regardless of the measurement
uncertainty. If the measured value is above (below) the limit line, the test is not
passed - ref IEC Guide 115:2007. The instrumentation accuracy is within limits
agreed by IECEE-CTL.

INSULATION DIAGRAM CONVENTIONS and GUIDANCE:
A measured value must be provided in the value columns for the device under evaluation. The symbol >
(greater than sign) must not be used. Switch-mode power supplies must be re-evaluated in the device under
evaluation therefore N/A must not be used with a generic statement that the component is certified.
Insulation diagram is a graphical representation of equipment insulation barriers, protective impedance and
protective earthing. If feasible, use the following conventions to generate the diagram:
- All isolation barriers are identified by letters between separate parts of diagram, for example separate
transformer
windings, optocouplers, wire insulation, creepage and clearance distances.
- Parts connected to earth with large dots are protectively earthed. Other connections to earth are functional
- Applied parts are extended beyond the equipment enclosure and terminated with an arrow.
- Parts accessible to the operator only are extended outside of the enclosure, but are not terminated with an
arrow.

supply frequency, at 110 % of the max RATED MAINS VOLTAGE, and after relevant tests of Clause 11.6 (i.e.,
overflow, spillage, leakage, ingress of water and particulate matter, cleaning & disinfection, & sterilization).
- Measurements were taken for both AC and DC, measurements shown are the worst case with regards to
switches in the test setup.
1) The measured leakage currents did not exceed allowable limits. The measured value shows the maximum
value for the combination of switches, S5 and S12.
2) Testing was conducted without / with a non-frequency-weighted device (NFWD).

Clause Remarks Information/Comments
4.10 Power supply The model DEPS9601A has two inputs; the one is a
separate input with the appliance inlet for connection to
100-240 Vac mains supply, and the other one is a
separate input of 24 Vdc.
The model DEPS9601A does not supply AC and DC
power source at the same (AC power source is prior to
DC power supply) although two differnet power sources
are connected to the equipment simultanously. The model
DEPS9601A supplies DC power source only when AC
source is not connected.
5.3 Ambient temperature, humidity,
atmospheric pressure
Temperature: (10 – 35) °C
Humidity: (30 – 75) % R.H.
Pressure: (700 – 1060) hPa
The manufacturer recommended operating ambient is
35 °C.
However, the equipment has been evaluated for “safety”
at 40 °C and complies with this standard.
6 Classification of ME equipment
and ME systems
Equipment has been evaluated as Class I, continuous
operation, ordinary equipment and has not been
evaluated for use in the presence of flammable
anaesthetic mixture with air, oxygen, or nitrous oxide. The
equipment is not intended for patient connection.
8.5 Saperation of parts According to the insulation diagram of this equipment, the
equipment is provided with 2MOOP (DI/RI insulation)
between the primary and secondary (16 Vdc output).
For model DEPS9601A, there is no separation between
24 Vdc input and 16 Vdc output in the secondary circuit,
refer to insulation diagram (Area E) and associated table.
When it needs to be considered MOPP, additional testing
shall be conducted considering the end use specified by
the manufacturer.
Summary of compliance with National Differences (List of countries addressed):
See main test report No. 269183 and amendment test report No. 323575
The product fulfils the requirements of
- IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013

Calibration All instruments used in the tests given in this test report are calibrated and
traceable to national or international standards.
Further information about traceability will be given on request.
Measurement
uncertainty
Measurement uncertainties are calculated for all instruments and instrument
set-ups given in this report. Calculations are based on the principles given in
the standard EA-4/02 (Dec. 1999), IEC Guide 115:2007 and other relevant
internal Nemko-procedures.
Further information about measurement uncertainties will be given on request.
Evaluation of results If not explicitly stated otherwise in the standard, the test is passed if the measured
value is equal to or below (above) the limit line, regardless of the measurement
uncertainty. If the measured value is above (below) the limit line, the test is not
passed - ref IEC Guide 115:2007. The instrumentation accuracy is within limits
agreed by IECEE-CTL.

General product information:
This amendment test report No. 323575 shall always be used together with the main test report No.
269183.
The updates concerned in this test report are as follows;
- Modification of an address for testing lavoratory. No changes in its location (see bold letters on page 1
and 2).
- Modification of addresses for applicant, manufacturer and factory. No changes in its location (see bold
letters on page 1 and factory list, and copy of marking plate).
- Modification of trademark design (see page 1 and copy of marking plate).
- Addition of a variant model DEPS9601A with modification of input rating (see bold letters on page 1 and
copy of marking plate).
- Addition of DC input connector for model DEPS9601A (see copy of marking plate).
- Update the critical component list (see bold letters in appended table 8.10).
After reviewing of the above updates concerned, this amendment test report was checked with the following:
Cl. 4.11 Power input
Cl. 5.3 Ambient temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure
Cl. 5.7 Humidity preconditioning treatment
Cl. 8.8.3 Dielectric strength test

Model differences:
- The basic model is DEPS9601.
- The variant mode DEPS9601A is identical to basic model DEPS9601 escept for model type
designation and input rating
The variant model DEPS9601A has two inputs; the one is a separate input with the appliance inlet for
connection to 100-240 Vac mains supply, and the other one is a separate input of 24 Vdc.
When this equipment is installed on the end product, consideration must be given to the following;
- The leakage current test shall be checked in the end product.
- The DC power source has to be from the class I ME equipment complied with IEC 60601-1 in the end
product.
- The equipment was tested on a 20 A branch circuit. If used on a branch circuit greater than this, then
additional testing may be necessary.

Supplementary Information:
1) Test was conducted with model DEPS9601A.
2) Inside the appliance inlet between live pin and neutral pin before fuses.
3) There is a slot with > 1mm width.
4) Fault testing was conducted with short-circuited U4 (between 24 Vdc input and 16 Vdc output) to show no
hazard between circuits, therefore no distances were considered necessary. For the operator, the voltage
was less than 42.4 Vpeak or 60 Vdc in which performing voltage measurements in SFC. Compliance of
SIP/SOP has to be still evaluated for the end product application. The output circuits have not been
evaluated for direct patient connection. And also see summary of testing.
INSULATION DIAGRAM CONVENTIONS and GUIDANCE:
A measured value must be provided in the value columns for the device under evaluation. The symbol >
(greater than sign) must not be used. Switch-mode power supplies must be re-evaluated in the device under
evaluation therefore N/A must not be used with a generic statement that the component is certified.
Insulation diagram is a graphical representation of equipment insulation barriers, protective impedance and
protective earthing. If feasible, use the following conventions to generate the diagram:
- All isolation barriers are identified by letters between separate parts of diagram, for example separate
transformer windings, optocouplers, wire insulation, creepage and clearance distances.
- Parts connected to earth with large dots are protectively earthed. Other connections to earth are functional
- Applied parts are extended beyond the equipment enclosure and terminated with an arrow.
- Parts accessible to the operator only are extended outside of the enclosure, but are not terminated with an
arrow.
